items.title: Turning prompts into five scalable workflows with Figma Weave
items.summary: To build upon a visual language with AI, you need to harness the logic behind each prompt—building, editing, and directing how visuals come to life. Here, we share five creative workflows that show the breadth of what the Figma Weave canvas can do.

items.title: How Figma AI tools helped us bring extra delight to the canvas
items.summary: For our annual April Fun Day, we used Figma Make, Weave, and MCP to build six mini games that captured the spirit of play. Here’s what we learned.

items.title: Build with more context and more control in Figma Make
items.summary: Starting today, Make kits and Make attachments bring context into Figma Make, so prototypes start from real components, data, and constraints.

items.title: Figma's next-generation data caching platform
items.summary: Scalability and reliability gaps in our Redis platform were a growing threat to Figma's site availability. This is the story of how we built FigCache, a foundational technology that underpins a reimagined internal platform for ephemeral data.

items.title: Agents, meet the Figma canvas
items.summary: Starting today, you can use AI agents to design directly on the Figma canvas. And with skills, you can guide agents with context about your team’s decisions and intent.

items.title: Design’s influence is expanding, and here’s why that feels hard
items.summary: As AI accelerates creation and raises expectations, designers are navigating a field that’s bigger, faster, and more demanding than ever.

items.title: Issue no.15: The state of design
items.summary: AI-powered tools and workflows are fundamentally changing the way we design.

items.title: 5 design skills to sharpen in the AI era
items.summary: Our latest research highlights the skills designers need to meet the moment.

items.title: Our Config 2026 speakers on the biggest opportunities with AI
items.summary: Here’s a first look at our speakers and how they’re interrogating craft, quality, and intention in an AI-powered world.

items.title: Meet the maker: Kelsey Fairhurst’s irresistibly bold flatware
items.summary: Brooklyn-based designer Kelsey Fairhurst launched Forks Plus, a “softline brutalist” flatware brand, with the help of Figma.

items.title: End-to-end lineage with DVC and Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow apps
items.summary: In this post, we show how to combine DVC (Data Version Control), Amazon SageMaker AI, and Amazon SageMaker AI MLflow Apps to build end-to-end ML model lineage. We walk through two deployable patterns — dataset-level lineage and record-level lineage — that you can run in your own AWS account using the companion notebooks.

items.title: Accelerate Generative AI Inference on Amazon SageMaker AI with G7e Instances
items.summary: Today, we are thrilled to announce the availability of G7e instances powered by NVIDIA RTX PRO 6000 Blackwell Server Edition GPUs on Amazon SageMaker AI. You can provision nodes with 1, 2, 4, and 8 RTX PRO 6000 GPU instances, with each GPU providing 96 GB of GDDR7 memory. This launch provides the capability to use a single-node GPU, G7e.2xlarge instance to host powerful open source foundation models (FMs) like GPT-OSS-120B, Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B (NVFP4 variant), and Qwen3.5-35B-A3B, offering organizations a cost-effective and high-performing option.

items.title: ToolSimulator: scalable tool testing for AI agents
items.summary: You can use ToolSimulator, an LLM-powered tool simulation framework within Strands Evals, to thoroughly and safely test AI agents that rely on external tools, at scale. Instead of risking live API calls that expose personally identifiable information (PII), trigger unintended actions, or settling for static mocks that break with multi-turn workflows, you can use ToolSimulator’s large language model (LLM)-powered simulations to validate your agents. Available today as part of the Strands Evals Software Development Kit (SDK), ToolSimulator helps you catch integration bugs early, test edge cases comprehensively, and ship production-ready agents with confidence.

items.title: Omnichannel ordering with Amazon Bedrock AgentCore and Amazon Nova 2 Sonic
items.summary: In this post, we’ll show you how to build a complete omnichannel ordering system using Amazon Bedrock AgentCore, an agentic platform, to build, deploy, and operate highly effective AI agents securely at scale using any framework and foundation model and Amazon Nova 2 Sonic.

items.title: Introducing granular cost attribution for Amazon Bedrock
items.summary: In this post, we share how Amazon Bedrock’s granular cost attribution works and walk through example cost tracking scenarios.

items.title: Optimize video semantic search intent with Amazon Nova Model Distillation on Amazon Bedrock
items.summary: In this post, we show you how to use Model Distillation, a model customization technique on Amazon Bedrock, to transfer routing intelligence from a large teacher model (Amazon Nova Premier) into a much smaller student model (Amazon Nova Micro). This approach cuts inference cost by over 95% and reduces latency by 50% while maintaining the nuanced routing quality that the task demands.

items.title: Power video semantic search with Amazon Nova Multimodal Embeddings
items.summary: In this post, we show you how to build a video semantic search solution on Amazon Bedrock using Nova Multimodal Embeddings that intelligently understands user intent and retrieves accurate video results across all signal types simultaneously. We also share a reference implementation you can deploy and explore with your own content.

items.title: Nova Forge SDK series part 2: Practical guide to fine-tune Nova models using data mixing capabilities
items.summary: This hands-on guide walks through every step of fine-tuning an Amazon Nova model with the Amazon Nova Forge SDK, from data preparation to training with data mixing to evaluation, giving you a repeatable playbook you can adapt to your own use case. This is the second part in our Nova Forge SDK series, building on the SDK introduction and first part, which covered kicking off customization experiments.

items.title: From hours to minutes: How Agentic AI gave marketers time back for what matters
items.summary: In this post, we share how AWS Marketing’s Technology, AI, and Analytics (TAA) team worked with Gradial to build an agentic AI solution on Amazon Bedrock for accelerating content publishing workflows.

items.title: Cost-efficient custom text-to-SQL using Amazon Nova Micro and Amazon Bedrock on-demand inference
items.summary: In this post, we demonstrate two approaches to fine-tune Amazon Nova Micro for custom SQL dialect generation to deliver both cost efficiency and production ready performance.
